rationale: Approved CAR-T + ADC; classical B-cell surface marker
Approved Mirvetuximab (ELAHERE); GPI-anchored on outer leaflet
TROP-2-adjacent validated tumor antigen; large mucin ECD; multiple antibody programs
Invariant chain on B/dendritic cell surface; clinical-stage Milatuzumab ADC

Single-pass type I TM glycoprotein with a large extracellular domain. Canonical residence is melanosomal (lysosome-related organelle), but the protein transits the PM during melanosome biogenesis and surface biotinylation / flow cytometry / HMB-45 IHC detect a minority PM pool on melanocytes and melanoma cells — gene-specific evidence for `contextual / dual_localization`. The separate tebentafusp / gp100·HLA pMHC story does not affect this verdict either way (pMHC is not credited for protein-body surface accessibility, but PMEL has its own documented PM transit independent of MHC presentation).
Apical phosphate transporter; clinical Lifastuzumab vedotin ADC

Cancer-testis antigen; intracellular at baseline. Clinical T-cell engagers (brenetafusp / IMC-F106C) target the HLA-A*02-restricted peptide, but the protein body is not surface — `no` / `pmhc_only_intracellular`.
ER-resident at baseline; reaches outer leaflet during immunogenic cell death
Cytoplasmic at baseline; tumor-stress-induced surface presentation
ER chaperone at baseline; ER-stress/oncogenic-state translocation to outer leaflet
Membrane-anchored on the cytoplasmic face; tests "membrane = surface" trap

Classical lysosomal membrane glycoprotein; reaches plasma membrane via lysosomal exocytosis (CD107a degranulation marker on NK / cytotoxic T cells, surface staining on metastatic tumor cells). Conditional surfacing puts it in the contextual class.
Polytopic TM but lysosomal; TM topology does not imply PM accessibility
Imaged-confirmed signaling endosome; M1 false positive
Cycles between Golgi/endosomes/autophagosomal precursors; multi-pass TM but never PM in steady state
TGN46 — **cycles continuously between TGN and PM** via the secretory recycling pathway; canonical marker for the TGN-PM trafficking cycle. Transient but routine surface presence.
TCR-T + bispecific: GSK4427296 TCR-T + AMG509 bispecific. 6-pass TM with small ECLs — also tests minimal_ectoloops case

KLK2 (hK2) — kallikrein-family secreted serine protease with no TM/GPI of its own. Surface accessibility is real but context-dependent: documented surface association on prostate epithelium and stable deposition on the sperm plasma membrane during seminal transit, plus PSMA-axis prostate-cell-surface co-targeting in clinical programs. Web-enabled triage runs across multiple models converge on contextual, never yes — the surface pool is tissue/cell-type restricted, not a canonical extracellular-face receptor.
CAR-T + ADC + bispecific: BNT211 CAR-T (BioNTech), AMG794 bispecific, multiple ADCs. Tight-junction claudin
approved bispecific + CAR-T: Talquetamab approved 2023 (multiple myeloma); orphan GPCR
approved bispecific + ADC: M9140 ADC; multiple approved/clinical programs
clinical CAR-T + ADC: Multiple PSCA programs; GPI-anchored
clinical ADC: ABBV-011 ADC (AbbVie SCLC)
clinical mAb: Vantictumab (OMP-18R5, Ipsen/OncoMed); 7TM Wnt receptor
clinical ADC: Multiple Wnt-receptor ADC programs
clinical ADC: PF-06647263 ADC

Epiregulin — EGF-family ligand. TM precursor transits PM transiently before ADAM/MMP shedding; dominant detected and biologically active form is the soluble shed ligand. Clinical anti-epiregulin mAb programs exist but target the shed soluble pool.
approved TKI + clinical ADC: Crizotinib/lorlatinib approved (small molecule); ADC programs in development
approved bispecific: Tarlatamab/Imdelltra approved 2024 SCLC
clinical CAR-T + ADC: Cirmtuzumab, Zilovertamab vedotin, Lyell CAR-T
clinical ADC: BA3021 conditionally-active ADC (BioAtla)
clinical mAb: Multiple anti-CCR8 mAb programs (Treg depletion)
clinical mAb: Soluble EphB4 fusion programs
clinical ADC + CAR-T: AMG553 CAR-T, FLT3xCD3 bispecifics
clinical ADC: Multiple GFRalpha-1 ADC programs
clinical ADC: Glembatumumab vedotin (Celldex)
clinical mAb: OTSA101-DTPA (radioimmunoconjugate)
clinical small molecule: Adenosine A3 receptor; multiple clinical programs
approved peptide drug: Tirzepatide (GIP/GLP-1 dual agonist) approved
clinical peptide drug: Multiple glucagon receptor programs
approved small molecule: Lorcaserin (was approved); 7TM serotonin receptor
approved peptide: Afamelanotide (Scenesse) approved; melanocortin
clinical small molecule: Bile acid receptor TGR5; multiple programs
clinical small molecule: Bradykinin B1 receptor; multiple programs
approved small molecule: H2 receptor (ranitidine/famotidine class)
clinical small molecule: Neuropeptide Y receptor 5; obesity programs
clinical small molecule: Metabotropic glutamate receptor 4; CNS programs
clinical small molecule: Vasopressin V1A receptor; conivaptan family

Proto-oncogene tyrosine kinase; canonically myristoylated/palmitoylated to inner leaflet (cytoplasmic face). Ecto-SRC has been reported on cancer cell surfaces (melanoma, other tumors) — borderline conditional/pathological surfacing. M1 votes 3/6.
Rho GTPase; geranylgeranylated to inner leaflet. Cytoplasmic signaling.
Heterotrimeric G-alpha-q; lipidated cytoplasmic-side anchor.
G-protein beta-1 subunit; peripheral on inner leaflet via Gγ prenylation.

Src-family tyrosine kinase; canonically inner-leaflet myristoylated/palmitoylated. **Cell-surface LYN reported on B cells and in some cancer contexts**, parallel to ecto-SRC. Borderline pathological surfacing.
Hematopoietic cell kinase (Src family); inner-leaflet lipid-anchored.
Ras GTPase; lipidated inner leaflet (same family as KRAS in benchmark).
Rac1 GTPase; geranylgeranylated to inner leaflet.
Ribophorin 1; ER membrane subunit of OST glycosylation complex.
Ribophorin 2; ER membrane subunit of OST.
SREBP cleavage-activating protein; ER cholesterol sensor.
IP3 receptor type 3; ER membrane calcium channel.
IP3 receptor type 1; ER membrane calcium channel.
Sec61 translocon γ subunit; ER protein-translocation channel.

β-1,4-galactosyltransferase 1; primarily Golgi-resident, but **a cell-surface form on sperm mediates zona pellucida adhesion**; also reported on tumor cells. Conditional/cell-type-restricted surfacing.

TMED9 p24-family cargo receptor; ER ↔ ERGIC ↔ cis-Golgi cycling for traffic of GPI-anchored cargo. Golgi-residence dominant; no documented stable PM pool. Adversarial M1 hit testing whether secretory-pathway cycling is conflated with PM accessibility.

TMED10 p24-family cargo receptor — same ER-Golgi cycling pattern as TMED9 but with documented minority PM pool from secretory cycling and reports of cell-surface display in some cancer contexts. Distinguishable from TMED9 by the more frequent surface-detection reports; dual-localization with Golgi-dominant residence + minority PM fraction qualifies for contextual / dual_localization.
N-acetylgalactosaminyltransferase 1; Golgi glycosyltransferase.
Nuclear pore complex transmembrane component; nuclear envelope.
SUN domain protein 1; inner nuclear membrane (LINC complex).
SUN domain protein 2; inner nuclear membrane.
Nesprin-1; outer nuclear membrane (LINC complex), cytoskeleton-coupled.
Nesprin-2; outer nuclear membrane.

Mitochondrial outer-membrane porin; **plasma-membrane VDAC1 (pl-VDAC) is well-documented** in B cells, neurons, sperm, cancer cells, erythrocytes. Anti-VDAC1 antibodies in development. Borderline due to dual-localization.

Pro-apoptotic Bcl-2 family; canonically translocates to mitochondrial outer membrane during apoptosis. **Surface BAX has been reported as an apoptotic-cell marker** (parallel to CALR's ICD-induced surface translocation).

TGF-β1 is secreted as a precursor cleaved into LAP + mature TGF-β1 (small latent complex). On Tregs / platelets the complex is **covalently disulfide-bonded to surface GARP (LRRC32)** via specific intermolecular cysteine pairs, co-trafficked from the ER as a single unit. Livmoniplimab (ABBV-151) is a clinical antibody specifically targeting the GARP:latent-TGF-β1 complex. Covalent surface attachment is the right mechanism category.

Complement C3 secreted plasma protein; cleavage fragment **C3b is covalently deposited on cell surfaces during opsonization**. Borderline because C3 itself is soluble but its activation product is surface-anchored.
Transferrin; secreted iron-transport plasma protein.
Interleukin-6; secreted cytokine.
Insulin-like growth factor 1; secreted hormone.

Prothrombin secreted coagulation zymogen. Recruits to activated-platelet surfaces during prothrombinase-complex assembly via Gla-domain binding to phosphatidylserine, but **the protein has no membrane anchor of its own** — surface association is via Ca²⁺-dependent reversible binding, not membrane insertion. Recruitment ≠ surface accessibility.

Fibronectin secreted ECM glycoprotein. Forms cell-surface fibronectin matrix via integrin α5β1 binding (recruitment), and EDB-fibronectin antibodies target tumor-stromal fibronectin, but **the protein has no membrane anchor of its own** — it's a soluble protein that binds surface receptors. Recruitment ≠ surface accessibility.
Apolipoprotein B-100; secreted on lipoprotein particles.
Vascular endothelial growth factor A; secreted angiogenic factor.
α2-macroglobulin; secreted plasma protease inhibitor.
JAK3 tyrosine kinase; cytoplasmic (associates with γc of cytokine receptors). Tofacitinib target.
Spleen tyrosine kinase; cytoplasmic. Fostamatinib target.
B-Raf serine/threonine kinase; cytoplasmic. **Vemurafenib/dabrafenib approved**. Tests "approved drug ⇒ surface" trap.
Bruton's tyrosine kinase; cytoplasmic. **Ibrutinib approved**.
JAK1 tyrosine kinase; cytoplasmic. Multiple approved JAK inhibitors.
JAK2 tyrosine kinase; cytoplasmic. **Ruxolitinib approved** (myelofibrosis).
TYK2 tyrosine kinase; cytoplasmic. Deucravacitinib approved (psoriasis).
AKT2 serine/threonine kinase; cytoplasmic. Capivasertib clinical/approved.
MEK1 kinase; cytoplasmic. **Cobimetinib/trametinib approved**.
MEK2 kinase; cytoplasmic.

Histone deacetylase 6; cytoplasmic/perinuclear with no membrane anchor. 'Extracellular HDAC6' reports are mostly vesicular cargo (in EVs, not cell-surface accessible) or recruitment-style binding. **No own membrane anchor** — recruitment ≠ surface accessibility. Multiple clinical small-molecule inhibitors target the intracellular catalytic pocket.
IKK-β; cytoplasmic. Multiple clinical NF-κB-pathway programs.
LRRK2 kinase; cytoplasmic/peripheral. **Parkinson's clinical programs**.
ER calcium sensor; canonically ER-resident, but **clusters at ER-PM junctions during store-operated calcium entry (SOCE)**; some PM staining reports. Borderline due to ER-PM junction localization.

Transferrin receptor 1 (CD71); classical iron-uptake receptor; **multiple clinical antibody programs** (CX-2029 EpCAM-conditional ADC, TROP-2/TFRC dual-targeting), plus the standard transferrin-conjugate brain-shuttle approaches. Type II single-pass TM with large ECD. Missed by SURFY and HPA despite being a canonical surface receptor.

β2-microglobulin; structural light chain of MHC class I (and CD1 family). **No anchor of its own**, but is stably present on the surface of every nucleated cell as the non-covalent partner of TM-anchored MHC-I heavy chain — assembled in the ER and co-trafficked. Canonical example of accessibility via stable complex partnership. Clinical anti-B2M programs exist (historical mAb work, current B2M-directed CAR-T approaches in multiple myeloma).

GARP — single-pass type I TM glycoprotein with a large LRR extracellular domain. Restricted expression on Tregs and platelets. Anchors latent TGF-β1 to the cell surface via covalent disulfide bonds. Clinical antibody program: **livmoniplimab (ABBV-151)** targets the GARP:latent-TGF-β1 complex in solid-tumor combination trials with anti-PD1.

Second canonical lysosomal membrane glycoprotein (CD107b). Reaches the PM via lysosomal exocytosis on activated cytotoxic lymphocytes / NK cells — same conditional-surfacing mechanism as LAMP1. Surface staining is a marker of degranulation.

Olfactory receptor 1A1 — classical 7TM rhodopsin-family GPCR. Stably surface on olfactory sensory neurons (and reported in some ectopic-expression contexts). Expression is tissue-restricted to a specialized cell type but the surface architecture is unambiguous when expressed.

Desmoglein 3 — single-pass type I TM cadherin in desmosomes of basal keratinocytes and oral mucosa. **Pemphigus vulgaris autoantigen** — patient autoantibodies bind cell-surface DSG3 on keratinocytes (validation of surface accessibility). Tissue-restricted expression.

Glycophorin A (MNS blood group antigen) — single-pass type I TM sialoglycoprotein on the erythrocyte surface. Classical surface architecture; RBC-restricted expression. Targeted by anti-GYPA in blood-group serology and as an RBC marker in clinical immunology.

Cellular prion protein — canonical GPI-anchored outer-leaflet protein, abundant on neurons. Dominant localization is the plasma membrane; intracellular pools are routine biosynthesis / endolysosomal recycling. Anti-PRNP antibodies recognize the surface form clinically; conformational variant PrP^Sc is the disease antigen.

NRROS (legacy alias LRRC33) — single-pass type I TM glycoprotein with a large LRR extracellular domain on myeloid cells (microglia, monocytes); the myeloid-cell analog of GARP / LRRC32 on Tregs. Anchors latent TGF-β1 to the cell surface via covalent disulfide bonds; clinical antibody programs target the NRROS:latent-TGF-β1 complex in CNS / myeloid contexts.

HSP60 — canonically mitochondrial matrix chaperonin. **Reported on the cell surface** in tumor cells, stress contexts, and as a damage-associated molecular pattern (DAMP). Mechanism of surface translocation is debated (chaperone export, mitochondrial leakage, vesicular). Tests whether the model recognizes the minority-surface dual-localization pattern.

Mesothelin — GPI-anchored on the outer leaflet of mesothelial cells and overexpressed in mesothelioma / ovarian / pancreatic / lung adenocarcinoma. Clinical anti-MSLN ADC (anetumab ravtansine), CAR-T, and bispecific programs. 4/7 surface DB sources flag it; classical GPI mechanism.

Carcinoembryonic antigen (CEA) — GPI-anchored, oncofetal surface antigen on epithelial tumors. Tusamitamab ravtansine (anti-CEACAM5 ADC) in clinical trials; classical clinical surface target. 4/7 surface DB sources.

CD24 — small GPI-anchored sialoglycoprotein on B cells, neutrophils, and many tumors. Macrophage 'don't eat me' signal via Siglec-10. Clinical-stage anti-CD24 antibodies (e.g. SNS-101). 2/7 surface DB sources — disagreement-rich GPI.

Tetherin (BST-2 / CD317) — interferon-induced GPI-anchored antiviral. Unusual N-terminal TM + C-terminal GPI dual-anchor architecture but functionally outer-leaflet on the cell surface. 4/7 surface DB sources.

Renal dipeptidase — GPI-anchored ectoenzyme on kidney brush border, lung endothelium, and colon cancer. Clinical-stage anti-DPEP1 antibody (LSALT peptide / DC-101 platform). 4/7 surface DB sources; classical GPI.

Zona pellucida glycoprotein 3 — single-pass TM precursor at the oocyte surface; furin cleavage releases the ectodomain into the surrounding zona pellucida matrix during oogenesis. The TM-anchored precursor stage is the textbook `dual_localization` pattern (parallel to EREG): a TM precursor that transits the PM before regulated proteolysis releases the dominant form. The mature matrix-deposited ZP3 itself is `secreted_only` (no own anchor), but the documented TM-precursor stage on the oocyte PM is enough for `contextual / dual_localization`. Restricted to the female germline / oocyte lineage.

IZUMO1 — single-pass TM with Ig-like extracellular domain, required for sperm-egg fusion. **Sequestered in the acrosomal cap of spermatids at baseline; PM-exposed only after the acrosome reaction** (a sperm-specific cell-state event). Target of contraceptive immunology research. 2/7 surface DB sources.

Desmoglein 4 — type I single-pass TM cadherin restricted to hair follicle and stratified epithelia. Same classical TM cadherin architecture as DSG1/2/3 — tissue-restricted expression, classical surface mechanism where expressed. 3/7 surface DB sources.

DC-LAMP (CD208) — third member of the LAMP family. Single-pass TM glycoprotein on late-endosomal / lysosomal membranes in mature dendritic cells. Reaches the PM via lysosomal exocytosis during DC maturation and antigen presentation. 3/7 surface DB sources.

CD63 (LAMP-3 in old literature; tetraspanin TSPAN30) — multi-pass tetraspanin on late-endosomes / MVBs / lysosomes. Reaches the PM via lysosomal exocytosis and is a canonical exosomal-surface marker. Surface-displayed in activated platelets and degranulating immune cells. 4/7 surface DB sources.

Macrosialin / CD68 — single-pass TM sialomucin on macrophage / monocyte late-endosomes and lysosomes. Cycles to the PM during phagocytosis / lysosomal exocytosis. Used as a histological pan-macrophage marker; clinical anti-CD68 imaging agents in development. 3/7 surface DB sources.

LIMP-2 (SCARB2) — multi-pass TM lysosomal integral membrane protein, mannose-6-phosphate-independent receptor for β-glucocerebrosidase trafficking to lysosomes. Reaches the PM via lysosomal exocytosis; also the cellular entry receptor for enterovirus 71. 2/7 surface DB sources.

High mobility group box 1 — DNA-binding chromatin / nucleoplasmic protein. **Released extracellularly as a DAMP** during necrosis and active secretion, but the released form is soluble in extracellular space, not stably cell-surface anchored — fails the cardinal-rule wash test. Adversarial M1 hit (1 GO source mistakenly flags surface).

Heterogeneous nuclear ribonucleoprotein K — nucleoplasmic RNA-binding protein involved in pre-mRNA processing. No documented membrane anchor; ectopic surface reports in tumor proteomics studies are likely contamination. Adversarial M1 hit (1 CSPA mass-spec source).

ATP synthase F1 β subunit — primarily a mitochondrial matrix component of complex V. **Plasma-membrane 'ecto-F1-ATPase' is documented** on hepatocytes (HDL receptor), endothelium (apoA-I receptor), and multiple tumor lineages; multiple anti-ecto-ATP-synthase antibody / aptamer programs. Minority but stable PM pool alongside the dominant mitochondrial localization.

IZUMO4 — IZUMO family single-pass TM with Ig-like ECD; same acrosomal-cap → PM trafficking biology as IZUMO1 (sequestered in the acrosomal cap of spermatids at baseline; PM-exposed after the acrosome reaction). Forms heterodimer with IZUMO1 during sperm-egg fusion. 0/5 surface DB sources.

CRISP1 (AEG / DE) — CAP-superfamily glycoprotein secreted by epididymal epithelium that **stably attaches to the sperm plasma membrane during epididymal maturation in a wash-resistant manner** (mechanism candidates: GPI re-tethering, disulfide cross-linking, or very stable non-covalent association). Surface-accessible on ejaculated mature sperm through transit and fertilization; anti-CRISP1 contraceptive immunology programs. 0/5 surface DB sources.

Epithelial cell adhesion molecule — single-pass type I TM glycoprotein on epithelial cells. Cytokeratin-positive carcinoma marker; catumaxomab (anti-EpCAM x CD3 trifunctional, EMA-approved 2009 then voluntarily withdrawn 2017); solitomab and multiple EpCAM-targeted bispecifics / CAR-T programs clinical. 5/5 surface DB sources.

Melanoma chondroitin sulfate proteoglycan (CSPG4 / MCSP / NG2) — single-pass type I TM chondroitin-sulfate proteoglycan on melanoma, glioma, sarcoma. Clinical anti-CSPG4 CAR-T programs. 5/5 surface DB sources.

TROP-2 / TACSTD2 — single-pass type I TM glycoprotein on epithelial tumors. **Sacituzumab govitecan (Trodelvy) approved** 2020 for mTNBC and urothelial; datopotamab deruxtecan (Dato-DXd) approved 2024. 5/5 surface DB sources.

B7-H3 / CD276 — single-pass type I TM Ig-superfamily checkpoint molecule on tumor cells and tumor stroma. Multiple clinical ADC / CAR-T / radioligand programs (ifinatamab deruxtecan, omburtamab radioconjugate, mirzotamab clezutoclax). 4/5 surface DB sources.

CEACAM6 / NCA / CD66c — GPI-anchored carcinoembryonic-antigen family member on epithelial tumors. Multiple anti-CEACAM6 ADC / mAb / CAR-T programs (NEO-201, tinurilimab / BAY1834942). 4/5 surface DB sources.

MUC16 / CA-125 — single-pass type I TM with a massive O-glycosylated mucin ectodomain; ovarian-cancer serum biomarker and surface antigen. Oregovomab clinical; ubamatamab and multiple mucin-targeted T-cell engagers / ADCs. 4/5 surface DB sources.

Protein tyrosine kinase 7 — single-pass type I TM pseudokinase and Wnt-pathway co-receptor; oncofetal antigen on multiple carcinomas. Cofetuzumab pelidotin (PF-06647020) anti-PTK7 ADC clinical. 4/5 surface DB sources.

Claudin-18 isoform 2 (CLDN18.2) — 4-pass TM tight-junction claudin, gastric-restricted isoform. **Zolbetuximab (Vyloy) approved** 2024 for HER2-negative gastric / GEJ adenocarcinoma; multiple CAR-CLDN18.2 T-cell programs in clinic. Tissue-restricted to gastric epithelia but architectural surface story is unambiguous. 3/5 surface DB sources.

PSMA / folate hydrolase 1 — single-pass type II TM with a large extracellular glutamate-carboxypeptidase domain; prostate-cancer overexpressed. **Lutetium-PSMA-617 (Pluvicto) approved** 2022 for metastatic CRPC; multiple PSMA radioligands and PSMA-CAR-T programs. 3/5 surface DB sources.
Glypican-3 — GPI-anchored heparan sulfate proteoglycan on hepatocellular carcinoma. Codrituzumab (GC33) clinical mAb; multiple GPC3-CAR-T programs in clinical development. 3/5 surface DB sources.
Nectin-4 — single-pass type I TM Ig-superfamily adhesion molecule on urothelial / breast / lung cancers. **Enfortumab vedotin (Padcev) approved** 2019 for urothelial cancer. 3/5 surface DB sources.

Carbonic anhydrase IX — single-pass type I TM with extracellular PG + catalytic CA domain; hypoxia-induced HIF target on clear-cell RCC. **Girentuximab approved diagnostic** (PET imaging) 2024; therapeutic CA9-ADC and CAR-T programs clinical. 2/5 surface DB sources.

Delta-like non-canonical Notch ligand 1 (DLK1 / pref-1) — single-pass type I TM with EGF-like repeats; oncofetal antigen on neuroblastoma, hepatoblastoma, adrenal cortex carcinoma. ADCT-701 anti-DLK1 ADC clinical. ADAM17 ectodomain shedding releases a soluble form, but the TM precursor IS the surface form. 2/5 surface DB sources.
Glypican-2 — GPI-anchored heparan sulfate proteoglycan; oncofetal antigen on neuroblastoma. CHOP D3-based GPC2-CAR-T programs in clinical development. 2/5 surface DB sources.
LY6/PLAUR domain containing 1 — GPI-anchored LY6/uPAR-family surface protein; emerging tumor-associated antigen. 2/5 surface DB sources.

Somatostatin receptor 2 — 7TM rhodopsin-family GPCR on neuroendocrine tumors. **177Lu-DOTATATE (Lutathera) approved** 2018 for GEP-NETs; multiple SSTR-targeted radioligands and bispecifics. 2/5 surface DB sources.

Tyrosinase-related protein 1 — single-pass type I TM melanin-biosynthesis enzyme. **Canonical dominant compartment is the melanosome** (lysosome-related organelle); surface display on melanoma cells documented and clinically targeted (TA99 mAb, anti-TYRP1 ADC programs). Dual-localization biology: melanosomal-dominant with documented melanoma-cell-surface fraction. Borderline analog of PMEL but with cleaner surface evidence. 2/5 surface DB sources.

STEAP family member 2 — 6-pass TM metalloreductase / paralog of STEAP1; prostate-cancer overexpressed. Clinical STEAP2-targeted CAR-T and bispecific programs. **Not detected by any of the 5 retained M1 DBs** — adversarial M1 escape, tests whether the agent recognizes the STEAP1/STEAP2 architecture by family extrapolation. 0/5 surface DB sources.

Anti-Müllerian hormone receptor 2 — single-pass type I TM serine/threonine kinase receptor (TGF-β receptor superfamily). Surface on developing Müllerian-duct epithelium, granulosa / theca cells, and ovarian cancer. Clinical anti-AMHR2 ADC and CAR-T programs (e.g., murlentamab). Architectural surface story is unambiguous despite low DB cover. 2/5 surface DB sources (UP, SFY).

TM4SF1 / L6 — 4-pass TM tetraspanin-superfamily surface protein on lung / colorectal / pancreatic / breast carcinoma and tumor endothelium. Multiple clinical anti-TM4SF1 ADC programs. Same tetraspanin architecture as CD63 (also in benchmark) but more constitutively at the PM. 2/5 surface DB sources (UP, SFY).

ALPG (legacy alias ALPPL2) — germ cell alkaline phosphatase, GPI-anchored ectoenzyme. When expressed (testis, placenta, germ-cell tumors, oncofetal pattern), the protein is stably surface-displayed; expression itself is narrow / tissue-restricted (cancer-testis pattern). Multiple clinical anti-ALPPL2 ADC/CAR-T programs. contextual/tissue_restricted_surface captures the expression-restricted cancer-testis surface pattern.

LY6/uPAR family GPI-anchored cancer-testis antigen. Stable GPI display when expressed, but expression is narrow (testis/sperm and cancer cells). contextual/tissue_restricted_surface captures the expression-restricted cancer-testis surface pattern.

LYPD3 / C4.4A — LY6/PLAUR domain containing 3, uPAR paralog; GPI-anchored surface protein on lung / pancreatic / esophageal carcinomas. **Lupartumab amadotin (BAY1129980), anti-LYPD3 ADC clinical**. LY6-family GPI architecture is unambiguous. 2/5 surface DB sources (SFY, CSPA).
